description: Equirectangular planetary maps. Each entry has `body` + `variant`; the same body can appear multiple times (e.g. Earth blue_marble vs. sss_clouds). Entries with `asset_url` are auto-downloadable; others list `portal_url` for manual access. `provenance` notes the immediate host and the underlying agency/mission whose data produced the map.
license_notes: NASA imagery is public domain unless otherwise stated. ESA images are generally CC BY-SA 3.0 IGO. JAXA SELENE/Kaguya data carry JAXA-specific terms — see https://www.kaguya.jaxa.jp/en/ for use conditions. CNSA Chang'e data is distributed via NAOC public release terms. Textures sourced from Solar System Scope (via Wikimedia) are CC BY 4.0 by INOVE s.r.o., based on the cited agency data.

maps.mission: SDO / SOHO photospheric composites (Solar System Scope processing)
maps.description: Quiet-photosphere visual texture for rendering. Not a science map.
maps.provenance: Wikimedia Commons / Solar System Scope (INOVE s.r.o.)
maps.license: CC BY 4.0 (texture); underlying solar imagery NASA SDO / ESA-NASA SOHO
maps.citation: Solar System Scope (CC BY 4.0). Underlying data: NASA SDO / ESA-NASA SOHO photospheric imagery.
maps.note: The Sun has no meaningful sub-solar geometry; render with ambient=1.0.

maps.description: MESSENGER MDIS Best Direct Resolution (BDR) global monochrome basemap at 166 m/pixel (32 pixels per degree). Single-band reflectance from WAC 750 nm and NAC monochrome frames; the standard B&W albedo product.
maps.citation: USGS Astrogeology; MESSENGER MDIS team, NASA/JHU-APL. BDR (Best Direct Resolution) global monochrome basemap at 166 m/pixel, MDIS WAC/NAC 750 nm imaging.

maps.description: MESSENGER MDIS Enhanced Color global mosaic at 665 m/pixel (32 pixels per degree). Pseudo-color from a 3-band statistical stretch revealing compositional variation across Mercury’s surface.
maps.citation: Denevi, B. W. et al., USGS Astrogeology; MESSENGER MDIS team, NASA/JHU-APL. Enhanced color mosaic constructed from MDIS WAC filters 996/748/430 nm in PCA stretch.

maps.description: Color-enhanced MDIS global mosaic, processed by Solar System Scope from MESSENGER data
maps.provenance: Wikimedia Commons / Solar System Scope (INOVE s.r.o.)
maps.license: CC BY 4.0 (texture); underlying MESSENGER imagery is public domain (NASA/JHU-APL)
maps.citation: Solar System Scope (CC BY 4.0). Underlying data: Hawkins, S. E., et al. MESSENGER/MDIS team, NASA/JHU-APL.

maps.description: Full-resolution USGS MESSENGER MDIS basemap, accessible via Astropedia portal
maps.citation: USGS Astrogeology MESSENGER/MDIS basemap, v2.
maps.note: Direct asset URL is unstable. Download manually from the Astropedia portal.

maps.description: Cloud-top UV map of Venus's atmosphere
maps.provenance: Wikimedia Commons / Solar System Scope (INOVE s.r.o.)
maps.license: CC BY 4.0 (texture); underlying UV imagery NASA / ESA / JAXA
maps.citation: Solar System Scope (CC BY 4.0). Underlying data: Pioneer Venus / Venus Express / Akatsuki UV imaging.

maps.description: Magellan SAR global mosaic, processed by Solar System Scope
maps.provenance: Wikimedia Commons / Solar System Scope (INOVE s.r.o.)
maps.license: CC BY 4.0 (texture); underlying Magellan imagery is public domain (NASA/JPL)
maps.citation: Solar System Scope (CC BY 4.0). Underlying data: NASA Magellan Project; USGS Astrogeology.

maps.description: Cloud-free monthly composite, August 2004, with topography and bathymetry
maps.citation: Reto Stockli, NASA Earth Observatory / Blue Marble Next Generation, 2005.

maps.description: Cloud-free composite without bathymetry, 8192-wide TIFF
maps.citation: Reto Stockli, NASA Earth Observatory, Blue Marble Next Generation.

maps.mission: Earth cloud composite (Solar System Scope processing)
maps.description: Equirectangular cloud-cover albedo map, 8K
maps.provenance: Wikimedia Commons / Solar System Scope (INOVE s.r.o.)
maps.license: CC BY 4.0 (texture); underlying cloud imagery is public domain (NASA)
maps.citation: Solar System Scope (CC BY 4.0). Underlying data: NASA Earth Observatory cloud composites.

maps.mission: MODIS Blue Marble (Solar System Scope processing)
maps.description: SSS day-side Earth texture, 8K, smoothed for rendering
maps.provenance: Wikimedia Commons / Solar System Scope (INOVE s.r.o.)
maps.license: CC BY 4.0 (texture); underlying MODIS Blue Marble is public domain (NASA)
maps.citation: Solar System Scope (CC BY 4.0). Underlying data: NASA MODIS Blue Marble.

maps.mission: NASA Earth at Night (Solar System Scope processing)
maps.description: City lights / night-side Earth, 8K
maps.provenance: Wikimedia Commons / Solar System Scope (INOVE s.r.o.)
maps.license: CC BY 4.0 (texture); underlying VIIRS imagery is public domain (NASA/NOAA)
maps.citation: Solar System Scope (CC BY 4.0). Underlying data: NASA Earth at Night / Suomi-NPP VIIRS.

maps.description: Vivid stylised land/ocean colouring, no ice or clouds — brighter and more saturated than the photographic Blue Marble
maps.citation: Tom Patterson, Natural Earth III, www.shadedrelief.com/natural3, public domain.

maps.description: Clementine 750 nm albedo simple-cylindrical mosaic, low-res
maps.citation: Clementine mission (1994) UVVIS team; USGS Astrogeology.

maps.description: Global color albedo map (2019 release), 4K equirectangular
maps.citation: Ernie Wright, NASA Goddard SVS; LRO LROC WAC color mosaic.

maps.description: Global color albedo map (2025 release), 4K equirectangular
maps.citation: NASA Goddard SVS / LRO LROC team; based on >100,000 LROC WAC images.

maps.description: SSS lunar grayscale 8K texture
maps.provenance: Wikimedia Commons / Solar System Scope (INOVE s.r.o.)
maps.license: CC BY 4.0 (texture); underlying LROC data is public domain (NASA)
maps.citation: Solar System Scope (CC BY 4.0). Underlying data: NASA LRO LROC team.

maps.description: Chang'e-2 global lunar mosaic at 7 m/pixel
maps.license: CNSA / NAOC public release terms — see portal
maps.citation: Chinese National Space Administration (CNSA) / National Astronomical Observatories of China (NAOC).
maps.note: Requires registration on the Lunar and Planetary Data Release System (LPDR).

maps.description: Kaguya TC ortho-mosaic and DEM products (multi-resolution)
maps.license: JAXA SELENE data use conditions — see portal
maps.citation: JAXA, SELENE (Kaguya) project, Terrain Camera team.
maps.note: Requires JAXA DARTS / SELENE Data Archive registration in some cases.

maps.description: Mars global color/topography composite processed by Solar System Scope
maps.provenance: Wikimedia Commons / Solar System Scope (INOVE s.r.o.)
maps.license: CC BY 4.0 (texture); underlying MOLA/Viking imagery is public domain (NASA)
maps.citation: Solar System Scope (CC BY 4.0). Underlying data: NASA MGS MOLA team; Viking Orbiter; USGS Astrogeology.

maps.description: USGS Viking MDIM 2.1 color mosaic, 1 km/pixel — original Viking-only processing
maps.citation: USGS Astrogeology, Mars Viking MDIM 2.1 color mosaic (1 km/pixel). Underlying data: NASA Viking Orbiter (1976-80).

maps.description: Full-resolution USGS MDIM 2.1 global color mosaic at 232 m/pixel
maps.citation: USGS Astrogeology MDIM 2.1, based on Viking Orbiter imagery (1976-80).
maps.note: 12.7 GB. Disabled by default in fetch script; pass --include-large to download.

maps.description: HRSC global color mosaic at 50 m/pixel, distributed by DLR/FU Berlin
maps.license: ESA / DLR / FU Berlin, CC BY-SA 3.0 IGO
maps.citation: Gwinner, K., et al. (2016) Planetary and Space Science 126, 93-138.
maps.note: Distributed as tiled GeoTIFFs through the FU Berlin map server; no single-file equirectangular download. Manual stitching required.

maps.description: Tianwen-1 global Mars color mosaic released by CNSA in 2023
maps.citation: Tianwen-1 mission team, CNSA / NAOC, 2023.
maps.note: Distributed through the LPDR Mars data portal; registration required.

maps.description: Phobos global mosaic controlled by DLR using Viking and Mars Express HRSC
maps.provenance: Wikimedia Commons / DLR (Deutsches Zentrum für Luft- und Raumfahrt)
maps.license: Public Domain (NASA Viking imagery) / DLR processing
maps.citation: DLR Phobos mosaic, controlled with Viking Orbiter and ESA Mars Express HRSC.

maps.description: Cassini December 2000 Jupiter cylindrical mosaic (NASA Photojournal PIA07782)
maps.citation: NASA/JPL/Space Science Institute, Cassini ISS team, Photojournal PIA07782.

maps.description: Jupiter global color map (Cassini Dec 2000 flyby), processed by Solar System Scope
maps.provenance: Wikimedia Commons / Solar System Scope (INOVE s.r.o.)
maps.license: CC BY 4.0 (texture); underlying Cassini ISS imagery is public domain (NASA/JPL/SSI)
maps.citation: Solar System Scope (CC BY 4.0). Underlying data: NASA/JPL/Space Science Institute, Cassini ISS team.

maps.description: Io cylindrical map from Voyager imagery (NASA Photojournal PIA00319)
maps.citation: NASA/JPL, Photojournal PIA00319. Voyager Project, USGS.
maps.note: Image aspect 2.35:1 rather than exactly 2:1 — contains a thin annotated margin.

maps.description: Europa Voyager-Galileo SSI global mosaic (USGS), low resolution
maps.citation: USGS Astrogeology, Europa Voyager-Galileo SSI global mosaic.

maps.description: Ganymede global map by Björn Jónsson, derived from NASA Voyager and Galileo imagery

maps.citation: Map by Björn Jónsson. Underlying data: NASA/JPL Voyager 1/2 and Galileo missions.

maps.description: Callisto USGS global cylindrical map (small)

maps.citation: Solar System Scope (CC BY 4.0). Underlying data: NASA/JPL-Caltech/Space Science Institute.

maps.description: Enceladus global photomosaic (PIA14937, December 2011)

maps.description: Iapetus color global map from Cassini ISS

maps.note: No clean 2:1 equirectangular file at Wikimedia; download manually from USGS.

maps.license: CC BY 4.0 (texture); underlying Voyager 2 imagery is public domain (NASA/JPL)

maps.description: Triton global cylindrical map from Voyager 2 1989 flyby
maps.citation: NASA/JPL Voyager 2 imaging team (1989); USGS Astrogeology.

maps.description: Pluto global cylindrical map at 600 m/pixel from New Horizons July 2015 flyby

maps.description: Charon global cylindrical map from New Horizons (PS717 high-res, 180°-centered)

maps.description: Day-night hemisphere reference texture: white day hemisphere centred on lon 0 (+X), GREY shaded hemisphere centred on lon ±180 (grey, not black, so the night side stays legible), overlaid with a 30° graticule so the projected disk reads as a globe. Latitude-independent; render flat (no sun_direction / ambient=1.0) to verify which hemisphere a view_direction shows.
maps.citation: implanet synthetic reference texture.
